topic: Grid-scale battery storage costs and deployment, 2020-2024
sub_questions: What has been the trend in grid-scale battery storage costs from 2020 to 2024?
How much new battery storage capacity was deployed globally each year from 2020 to 2024?
What policy mechanisms have driven grid-scale battery storage deployment?
Which regions lead in grid-scale battery storage deployment capacity?

sources.title: Batteries and Secure Energy Transitions (IEA, 2024)
sources.snippet: Global battery storage capacity additions reached 130 GW in 2023, a record annual addition. The cost of utility-scale lithium-ion batteries fell below $150/kWh in 2023, representing a 90% decline from 2013 levels.

sources.title: Global Energy Storage Outlook (Wood Mackenzie, 2024)
sources.snippet: China accounted for over 60% of global battery storage deployments in 2023. The US Inflation Reduction Act provided significant tax incentives that accelerated domestic battery manufacturing and deployment.

sources.title: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Plus (2024)
sources.snippet: The levelized cost of storage (LCOS) for utility-scale lithium-ion four-hour systems fell to $60-120 per MWh in 2024. Wind and solar paired with storage are now cost-competitive with new gas peaker plants in most markets.

topic: Large language model compute efficiency trends, 2022-2024
sub_questions: How have training compute requirements for state-of-the-art LLMs changed from 2022 to 2024?
What techniques have most improved inference efficiency for large language models?
How has quantization affected the cost of deploying large language models?
What is the relationship between model size and performance per unit of compute?

sources.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models (Hoffmann et al., 2022)
sources.author: Jordan Hoffmann et al.
sources.snippet: Current large language models are significantly undertrained relative to what is compute-optimal. For a given compute budget, training a smaller model for more steps is more efficient. The Chinchilla 70B model outperforms GPT-3 175B using the same training compute.

sources.title: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale (Dettmers et al., 2022)
sources.author: Tim Dettmers et al.
sources.snippet: 8-bit quantization reduces LLM memory requirements by roughly 50% with less than 1% degradation in perplexity for models up to 175B parameters. This enables running 175B parameter models on consumer-grade GPU hardware.

sources.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models (Touvron et al., 2023)
sources.author: Hugo Touvron et al.
sources.snippet: Llama 2 models, trained on 2 trillion tokens, match or exceed GPT-3.5 on many academic benchmarks. The 13B Llama 2 model achieves performance competitive with 175B GPT-3 on several reasoning tasks.

topic: FDA approval of CRISPR gene therapy Casgevy for sickle cell disease, December 2023
sub_questions: What did the FDA approve for sickle cell disease in December 2023?
What makes Casgevy's mechanism novel and who is eligible to receive it?
What is the cost and commercial context of the new gene therapies?

sources.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapies to Treat Patients with Sickle Cell Disease (FDA, 2023)
sources.snippet: On December 8, 2023, the U.S. FDA approved two milestone treatments, Casgevy and Lyfgenia, the first cell-based gene therapies for sickle cell disease in patients 12 years of age and older. Casgevy is the first FDA-approved treatment to use CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing. Sickle cell disease is an inherited blood disorder affecting approximately 100,000 people in the U.S., most commonly African Americans.

sources.title: Vertex and CRISPR Therapeutics Announce US FDA Approval of CASGEVY (CRISPR Therapeutics, 2023)
sources.snippet: Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) was co-developed by Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics. Approximately 16,000 patients 12 years of age and older with severe sickle cell disease may be eligible for this one-time treatment.

sources.title: Casgevy: First CRISPR gene-editing treatment approved in U.S. (CNBC, 2023)
sources.snippet: Casgevy, co-developed by Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics, will cost $2.2 million per patient. CRISPR, the gene-editing technology, won its inventors the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2020.

topic: Semaglutide cardiovascular outcomes in the SELECT trial for obesity without diabetes
sub_questions: How was the SELECT cardiovascular outcomes trial designed?
What were the primary cardiovascular results for semaglutide 2.4 mg?
What population did the trial enroll and who sponsored it?

sources.title: Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Obesity without Diabetes (NEJM)
sources.snippet: The SELECT trial was a multicenter, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled, event-driven superiority trial. It enrolled patients 45 years of age or older who had preexisting cardiovascular disease and a body-mass index of 27 or greater but no history of diabetes. Patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to once-weekly subcutaneous semaglutide 2.4 mg or placebo.

sources.title: SELECT trial registry entry NCT03574597 (ClinicalTrials.gov)
sources.snippet: Trial NCT03574597 was a Phase 3 interventional study with 17,604 participants, sponsored by Novo Nordisk. It started on October 24, 2018 and was completed on June 29, 2023.

sources.title: Novo Nordisk: Semaglutide 2.4 mg cardiovascular outcomes data (GlobeNewswire, 2023)
sources.snippet: Semaglutide 2.4 mg delivered a statistically significant 20% risk reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE), with reductions demonstrated consistently across age, gender, ethnicity and starting BMI. Risk reductions in MACE were evident soon after initiation.
